category: Santa Claus - CAH Style v4.0 (MAINSTREAM REFERENCES ONLY)

jeopardy_principles: Pinning - clue must lead to only ONE answer
Give enough detail to clearly point at the answer
Avoid ambiguity - make the connection obvious
cah_principles: Absurdity through juxtaposition, not random shock
Clever connection between setup and punchline
mainstream_only: Apps: Amazon, Netflix, TikTok, Instagram, Uber, DoorDash, Venmo
Tech: iPhone, Alexa, Google, Facebook/Meta, Tesla
People: Trump, Biden, Taylor Swift, Elon Musk, Kardashians
Events: COVID-19, January 6th, student loans, inflation
Brands: Starbucks, Target, Walmart, McDonald's, Chipotle
Memes: Karen, OK Boomer, main character energy, rizz, slay

sample_questions.clue: This retail giant is where Santa actually gets those 'handmade' North Pole toys for $3.99 with free 2-day shipping
sample_questions.why_it_works: Everyone knows Amazon + Prime shipping. Clear juxtaposition of 'handmade' myth vs reality

sample_questions.clue: What Mrs. Claus caught Santa watching at 3am when he said he was 'checking the naughty list'
sample_questions.why_it_works: Universal reference, clear setup/punchline, obvious connection

sample_questions.clue: The streaming platform responsible for kids now believing in the Grinch more than Santa
sample_questions.why_it_works: Everyone knows Netflix, clear reference to streaming culture

sample_questions.clue: This is what Rudolph's glowing red nose is actually from, according to Mrs. Claus
sample_questions.why_it_works: Clear visual joke everyone gets - red nose = drug reference

sample_questions.clue: The app where influencers post their 'authentic' Christmas morning in matching pajamas that took 47 takes
sample_questions.why_it_works: Everyone knows Instagram influencer culture + performative authenticity

sample_questions.clue: What Santa really means when he asks if you've been 'naughty or nice' after his fourth glass of eggnog
sample_questions.why_it_works: Mall Santa + alcohol = obvious inappropriate behavior connection

sample_questions.clue: The social media platform where your drunk uncle will explain the War on Christmas during dinner
sample_questions.why_it_works: Everyone has a Facebook uncle + War on Christmas is mainstream controversy

sample_questions.clue: This billionaire's electric sleigh would actually make Santa's job easier if it didn't keep catching fire
sample_questions.why_it_works: Everyone knows Tesla fire problems + Elon Musk

sample_questions.clue: What kids in 2024 asked Santa for instead of toys: therapy, student loan forgiveness, and this other financial miracle
sample_questions.why_it_works: Millennial/Gen Z financial crisis is mainstream news

sample_questions.clue: The gig economy app Santa drives for after Christmas to pay off his North Pole credit card debt
sample_questions.why_it_works: Everyone knows Uber + gig economy struggles

sample_questions.clue: This pandemic turned Christmas 2020 into Zoom calls with grandma who doesn't know how to unmute
sample_questions.why_it_works: Universal COVID Zoom experience everyone lived through

sample_questions.clue: The payment app where you can see your friends Venmoing each other for 'Christmas gifts' with emoji codes
sample_questions.why_it_works: Venmo's public feed is well-known + emoji drug codes meme

sample_questions.clue: This is what the Elf on the Shelf is really training your kids to accept as normal surveillance
sample_questions.why_it_works: Post-Snowden everyone knows NSA + Elf watching parallel

sample_questions.clue: The celebrity who makes $3 million every December by defrosting for 'All I Want for Christmas'
sample_questions.why_it_works: The 'Mariah Carey defrost' meme is everywhere every December

sample_questions.clue: What Target moms call the Christmas shopping trip where they went for wrapping paper and spent $347
sample_questions.why_it_works: Target overspending is a universal meme everyone knows

sample_questions.clue: This coffee chain's cup design became the frontline of the War on Christmas according to your Facebook feed
sample_questions.why_it_works: 2015 red cup controversy was huge news + ongoing meme

sample_questions.clue: The fast food app Santa uses to DoorDash McDonald's at 2am on Christmas Eve
sample_questions.why_it_works: Everyone knows DoorDash + late night fast food ordering

sample_questions.clue: This is what killed more family Christmas dinners than politics: arguments about getting this shot
sample_questions.why_it_works: Vaccine debates at family dinners were universal 2020-2023 experience

sample_questions.clue: The AI assistant that heard everything you said around the Christmas tree and is definitely selling that data
sample_questions.why_it_works: Alexa privacy concerns are mainstream + everyone knows Amazon Echo

sample_questions.clue: What Gen Z calls it when they film their gift reactions for content instead of just saying thanks
sample_questions.why_it_works: Main character energy is a huge Gen Z meme + performative behavior

obscure_references: ❌ Deep Reddit memes only chronically online people know
❌ Obscure indie game or anime references
❌ References to things from before 2000 unless iconic
random_shock: ❌ Just saying 'penis' or 'butthole' without a setup
❌ Crude words with no logical connection to the clue
❌ Shock for shock's sake with no joke

before_using_reference: ✅ Would my parents know what this is?
✅ Has this been on mainstream news/TV?
✅ Would someone who doesn't use social media recognize this?
✅ Is this a brand/app/person with millions of users/followers?
✅ Has SNL or late night TV made jokes about this?
